danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy units displayed in red on tactical grid
Darkened/obscured map terrain until unit advances
danger_signals.meaning: Unknown threats ahead; enemy positions concealed

danger_signals.visual_form: Named antagonists like Raydrik, Veld appear as unique sprites
danger_signals.meaning: Critical threat; defeating this unit advances objective

danger_signals.visual_form: Character fatigue meter increases with each action
danger_signals.meaning: Character approaching unavailability; unit cannot deploy next chapter if fatigued

danger_signals.visual_form: Mixed enemy unit types (axemen, mounted units, magic users) positioned strategically

safety_signals.visual_form: Playable units displayed in blue on tactical grid
Marked structures on map that units can enter
safety_signals.meaning: Safe haven; grants items, currency, or health recovery

safety_signals.visual_form: Highlighted grid squares showing unit's possible movement
safety_signals.meaning: Strategic safety; unit can reach these positions within turn

safety_signals.visual_form: Magic user class unit with healing capabilities
safety_signals.meaning: Damage mitigation available; support unit can restore ally health

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Village of Fiana as home base; liberated towns show Thracia control

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Imperial insignia on enemy units and commanders
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Raydrik's forces (Duke of Munster occupation); Veld's Loptous Sect troops
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Occupied settlements; fortified positions; enemy encampments

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Veld and cult-aligned units possess dark magic visual effects
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Transformation into undead (Deadlord Mus); corrupted units
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Magical corruption; unholy sites; Veld's strongholds

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early game: Thracia under occupation by Grannvale
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Red enemy units dominate maps; blue allied forces small and scattered; towns show occupied status
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively hunt protagonist; bandits and military patrols present; allies are scattered refugees

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Mid-game: Leif rallies growing rebel army
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increasing blue units on map; rescued allies gain playable status; liberated towns switch allegiance visualization
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rescue objectives become primary; player army grows larger; enemies become more desperate

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Critical confrontation: Raydrik defeated, Veld transformation
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss unit transforms into undead Deadlord Mus; dark visual effect signals supernatural threat escalation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unit behavior changes from standard combat to possessed/corrupted attack patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Maps show blue-controlled territories; enemy presence reduced to remaining Grannvale holdouts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Defense posture; holding fortified positions against encroaching imperial forces

environmental_storytelling.technique: Map objective variety signals narrative progression
environmental_storytelling.example: Early chapters: 'Defeat bandits' (lawless state). Mid-chapters: 'Rescue hostages' (occupied territories). Late chapters: 'Defend position' (liberated kingdom consolidation)
Flat plains and roads belong to Grannvale-controlled lowlands; mountains represent refuges and liberated zones; villages show damage state reflecting occupation
Red units garrison towns and crossroads in early game; blue units appear only in wilderness/villages as Leif's hidden bases; shift marks territorial shift

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character class distribution shows military doctrine
environmental_storytelling.example: Grannvale heavily uses mounted units (imperial cavalry); rebel forces mix thieves and light units (ambush/guerrilla tactics); Loptous uses magic units (dark sorcery)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Limited movement options; unique class with restricted capabilities; must be protected
Expanded movement range; class evolution; commanders follow tactical orders
Positions near Leif defensively; veteran unit traits; tactical leadership cues
Aggressive enemy positioning early; initiates village assault; defeated but not final boss
Revealed late; uses dark magic; transforms defeated Raydrik into Deadlord corruption
Guard positions; patrol routes; some flee when outmatched; class-appropriate tactics
Cannot be deployed in following chapter; visual indicator of resource limitation

notes: Thracia 776 communicates world-state primarily through unit coloring (blue/red), map objectives that shift from survival to liberation to defense, and unit availability systems (fatigue). The generational gap narrative with parent title Genealogy of the Holy War creates layered political context: Leif fights to restore a fallen kingdom (Thracia) while cousin Seliph prepares against Arvis, both against the Grannvale Empire. Environmental storytelling relies on town occupation states, terrain types reflecting faction control, and the transformation of Raydrik into undead (Deadlord Mus) to signal supernatural/cult influence escalation. The fatigue system uniquely communicates consequence and resource scarcity—overuse of units in current chapter directly impacts roster availability, creating tension between tactical needs and long-term party management. No explicit faction color palette beyond blue/red alignment; faction identity conveyed through unit classes, dialogue, and map positioning.
